root_cause: The web service had been deployed with RELEASE=v2, which contains a regression that makes readiness fail and returns HTTP 500 on /orders. The database and payments service were healthy; the failure was isolated to the web container's bad release configuration.
actions_taken: Inspected running Docker containers, compose state, web/payments/load logs, and the live web environment. Confirmed sreft-web was running RELEASE=v2 while payments was on healthy RELEASE=v1. Verified /orders returned HTTP 500 before remediation. Updated the compose configuration for only the web service to RELEASE=v1 and ran docker compose up -d web to recreate only that container. Verified the recreated web container was running RELEASE=v1 and repeated /orders probes returned HTTP 200 with {"ok":true,"release":"v1"}.
postmortem: A bad web release reached production and caused checkout errors even though the container process stayed up. The safest remediation was a targeted rollback of the web service to the previous known-good release, leaving unrelated services and data untouched. Follow-up items are to strengthen pre-production coverage for /orders, require release health gates that exercise checkout behavior, and make rollback/runbook guidance explicit for release-specific regressions.
